title: Chromogeometry FAIL: GREEN_QUADRANCE_MISMATCH — C claimed as 2·b·e instead of 2·d·e
_fixture_purpose: Canonical confusion fail. Generator (b=1,e=1): the correct Green quadrance is C=Q_green(d,e)=2·d·e=2·2·1=4. A common error is to compute C=2·b·e=2·1·1=2 (using b instead of d). This fixture certifies that the validator catches this substitution error. Additionally, with C=2 and F=3, Pythagoras 4+9≠25 is also violated — both CG2 and CG5 should fire.

chromogeometry._error: WRONG: claimed C=2·b·e=2·1·1=2 instead of C=2·d·e=2·2·1=4

conic_classification.I_formula: I = |C − F| = |2 − 3| = 1 (but C is wrong; correct C=4 gives same I=1)
conic_classification._error: conic_type is wrong: claimed C=2 < F=3 → ellipse, but correct C=4 > F=3 → hyperbola

validation_checks.description: conic_type matches |C−F| and C vs F comparison
validation_checks.details: claimed C=2 < F=3 → ellipse; I=|2−3|=1 — internally consistent with wrong C
